design_rules: Clear CTA: 'Calculate yours free', 'Try the free calculator', 'Compare your numbers'
No clutter, no tiny text, no ugly screenshots, no generic stock photos

answer.description: Direct answer to a financial question
answer.example: How much house can you afford on $100K income? → $300K-$400K

comparison.example: Debt Snowball vs Avalanche: Which saves $2,347 more?

checklist.example: 7 numbers to check before buying a home in 2026

mistake.example: 5 mortgage mistakes that make a home $50K more expensive

first_10_pins.title: How Much House Can You Afford on $100K Income?
first_10_pins.subtitle: Typically $300K-$400K depending on down payment

first_10_pins.title: Debt Snowball vs Avalanche: Which Saves More?
first_10_pins.subtitle: Avalanche saves $2,347 more on a $30K debt. But snowball wins psychologically.

first_10_pins.title: 7 Numbers to Check Before Buying a Home
first_10_pins.subtitle: 1. Credit score 2. DTI ratio 3. Down payment 4. Emergency fund 5. Interest rate 6. Property tax 7. Insurance

first_10_pins.title: 5 Mortgage Mistakes That Cost $50K+
first_10_pins.subtitle: 1. Skipping rate shopping 2. Ignoring PMI 3. Not checking credit 4. Maxing budget 5. Forgetting closing costs
first_10_pins.cta: Avoid these — use our free calculator →

first_10_pins.title: $400K Home at 6.5% = $2,528/Month
first_10_pins.subtitle: That's $910,080 over 30 years. A 1% rate drop saves $92,000.

first_10_pins.title: $20K Loan at 8% for 5 Years = $405/Month
first_10_pins.subtitle: Total cost: $24,332. Payoff in 3 years instead? → $627/month, save $1,759.

first_10_pins.title: 401(k) vs Roth IRA: Which Grows More?
first_10_pins.subtitle: $500/month × 30 years: 401(k) = $611K pre-tax | Roth IRA = $509K tax-free

first_10_pins.title: 6 Numbers That Predict Your Financial Future
first_10_pins.subtitle: 1. Net worth 2. Savings rate 3. Debt-to-income 4. Credit score 5. Emergency fund months 6. Retirement number

first_10_pins.title: 4 Tax Mistakes Costing You Thousands
first_10_pins.subtitle: 1. Wrong W-4 2. Missing deductions 3. Not using tax-advantaged accounts 4. Underpaying estimated tax
